posts.text:
If you are counting Strait of Hormuz barrels, don’t forget to add the bypass pipelines if you want an apples-to-apples comparison to pre-war levels. 

What matters is not SoH flows, but Persian Gulf flows. As long as the bypass pipelines continue to be used (and they are), SoH
posts.summary: When measuring Persian Gulf oil exports, bypass pipelines that circumvent the Strait of Hormuz should be included in flow calculations to accurately compare current volumes to pre-war levels. The key metric for assessing regional oil supply is total Persian Gulf output rather than Strait of Hormuz transit alone, provided bypass pipeline usage remains consistent.

posts.text:
The Energy Department is delivering on President Trump’s Agenda to Reinvigorate the Nuclear Industrial Base:

Today, @DOE_EDF announced financing that will help rebuild America’s nuclear supply chain and accelerate the deployment of 10 new large-scale reactors. https://t.co/1IOyftvUHq
posts.summary: The Department of Energy announced financing to rebuild the U.S. nuclear supply chain and deploy 10 new large-scale reactors, which could reduce long-term demand for fossil fuels including oil as an energy source. However, the immediate oil market implications are unclear from the announcement alone, as nuclear power primarily competes with electricity generation rather than transportation fuels that drive crude oil demand.

posts.text:
Kpler's drone-based tank level data is sending a clear warning signal at cushing

The US crude market's most-watched inventory hub has shed around 11 million barrels since early May, and at the current draw pace, stocks are on course to test operational tank bottoms by mid-July. https://t.co/OHrryNIn62
posts.summary: Cushing crude inventory levels have fallen approximately 11 million barrels since early May and are projected to reach operational minimums by mid-July at the current depletion rate, according to Kpler's drone-based tank monitoring data. This significant drawdown at the US crude market's primary storage hub could constrain supply availability and potentially support crude oil prices.

posts.text: ALERT: #GasPrices appear to be rising to $3.99/gal in #Ohio today. This is a routine price cycle to an elevated amount due to the ongoing U.S./Iran situation.
posts.summary: Gas prices in Ohio are rising to $3.99 per gallon due to geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, reflecting a routine price cycle driven by market concerns over the situation.

posts.text:
Strait of Hormuz crossings rebound

Kpler’s rolling coverage of US-Iran diplomatic developments shows a clear shift in Strait of Hormuz activity across two consecutive weekends. Between 12–14 June, total crossings reached 32. One week later, from 19–21 June, crossings rose to 93 https://t.co/L03f6wFOx1
posts.summary: Strait of Hormuz crossings nearly tripled from 32 to 93 between mid-June and late June, coinciding with US-Iran diplomatic developments and suggesting a significant rebound in one of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoints.

posts.text: Today's technology-led selloff, which has seen the KOSPI tumble 10% and the Nasdaq retreat below 30,000, has triggered broad-based risk reduction across asset classes, including industrial metals. The weakness has been particularly pronounced in metals linked to the energy https://t.co/kU4N2ASfA3
posts.summary: A technology-led market selloff, marked by significant declines in major indices, has triggered broad risk reduction across asset classes including industrial metals linked to energy. The post suggests this risk-off sentiment is creating weakness in energy-related commodities, though specific oil market details are not provided in the visible text.

posts.text: ALERT: #GasPrices appear to be rising to $3.89/gal in #Florida today. This is a routine price cycle to an elevated amount due to the ongoing U.S./Iran situation.
posts.summary: Gas prices in Florida are rising to $3.89 per gallon due to geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, representing a routine price cycle to an elevated level rather than an anomalous spike.

posts.text: The Hormuz crisis is triggering a global race to build oil reserves. From India and Australia to Singapore and Pakistan, governments are expanding storage capacity to boost energy security, potentially creating demand for up to 1 billion barrels over the coming years. #Oil
posts.summary: Concerns over the Hormuz Strait are prompting multiple governments to significantly increase their oil storage capacity, which could generate demand for approximately 1 billion barrels of oil in the coming years. This strategic reserve buildup aims to enhance energy security across Asia and potentially support global oil markets through increased storage demand.

posts.text:
Iran Rushes to Woo Asia’s Largest Oil Importers After US Waiver

Sellers including middlemen and representatives from the National Iranian Oil Co. made contact with refiners in India, Japan, South Korea and elsewhere even before the license was officially granted, according to
posts.summary: Iran is actively reaching out to major Asian oil importers following a U.S. waiver, with oil sellers and National Iranian Oil Company representatives contacting refiners in India, Japan, and South Korea to resume or expand crude sales. The move signals Iran's attempt to quickly capitalize on improved access to key markets in the region.

posts.text: The Strait of Hormuz may be reopening, but tanker markets remain in crisis mode. Daily tanker rates have nearly doubled to over $190,000, while some VLCCs are reportedly earning up to $470,000 per day as vessel shortages persist. #Oil #Tankers #Hormuz #Shipping
posts.summary: Tanker rates are surging despite the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, with daily rates exceeding $190,000 and some large tankers earning up to $470,000 per day due to ongoing vessel shortages. The elevated shipping costs suggest continued market stress that could impact oil transport and pricing despite improved passage conditions.

posts.text: BRENT futures for deliveries next year are falling as traders anticipate there will be plenty of oil available to meet consumer requirements and start refilling depleted inventories. posts.text:
Global oil trade, including oil, condensate and refined products, is expected to rise by nearly 25% by 2050. 

Interregional crude and condensate trade stood at around 37.3 mb/d in 2025. By 2030, it is expected to increase to 41.8 mb/d, supported by rising oil demand in major https://t.co/qjExFbxenp
posts.summary: OPEC forecasts global oil trade to grow nearly 25% by 2050, with interregional crude and condensate trade increasing from 37.3 million barrels per day in 2025 to 41.8 mb/d by 2030, driven by rising oil demand in major markets.

posts.text:
Approvals for new gas-fired power plants rose to a 25-year high last year

Until 2023, most gas-fired power plant approvals were in gas-importing countries, but the US now dominates orders, driven by data centre demand

Learn more in our report ➡️ https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/DBX0xeI2as
posts.summary: Gas-fired power plant approvals reached a 25-year high in 2023, with the US driving growth due to data center demand, suggesting sustained long-term demand for natural gas rather than oil. This shift reflects how energy-intensive emerging industries are shaping fossil fuel infrastructure investments.

posts.text: UAE’s exit from OPEC+ reduced the group’s share of crude oil production and capacity. #TodayInEnergy https://t.co/n7FqyRemAh https://t.co/4c2K6dGU95
posts.summary: The UAE's departure from OPEC+ has reduced the organization's overall share of global crude oil production and capacity. This development diminishes OPEC+'s collective market influence and production control.

posts.text:
🚨 ETHANOL DEMAND 2026: The next leg of growth may not come from Washington.

Generational blending economics and global energy security concerns create fresh demand opportunities for US ethanol.

Our latest outlook examines where the gallons could actually come from. 👇
posts.summary: US ethanol demand growth in 2026 may be driven by favorable blending economics and global energy security needs rather than domestic policy support. This shift could create new market opportunities for American ethanol producers beyond traditional government incentives.

posts.text: US and Qatar claim EU methane rules will trigger gas supply crunch https://t.co/kY1awbwAxb
posts.summary: The US and Qatar contend that EU methane regulations could create gas supply shortages in Europe. This suggests potential market tightness and possible price pressures for natural gas supplies.

posts.text:
Global oil markets are facing a historic supply disruption amid the war in the Middle East, pushing up prices for consumers.

Our report sets out 10 immediate demand-side options to help governments, businesses & households ease the economic impacts → https://t.co/OiyU3jpxIq https://t.co/Ta9ZVNcA2O
posts.summary: The IEA warns that the Middle East conflict is causing a historic disruption to global oil supplies, which is driving up prices for consumers. The organization has released a report outlining 10 demand-side measures governments, businesses, and households can implement to mitigate the economic fallout.

posts.text: Renewable energy group to raise $3.6bn in China’s biggest IPO for 4 years https://t.co/MTq7WpF311
posts.summary: China's largest IPO in four years involves a renewable energy group raising $3.6 billion, signaling continued capital allocation toward clean energy development that could support China's energy transition away from fossil fuels. This investment trend may reduce future demand growth for oil as renewable capacity expands in the world's second-largest economy.

posts.text:
European gas markets don't move in isolation. Storage dynamics, shifting LNG flows, pipeline policy, geopolitical risk. It all connects, and it all moves fast.

EA Live Europe Gas brings you real-time updates and expert analysis from our global team, tracking how worldwide https://t.co/6LVy7dp4OY

The service aims to provide real-time tracking of these dynamic international energy flows.

posts.text: Interesting analysis from Goldman on EVs. They estimate total hit to global oil demand of 130,000-320,000 b/d by end-2027 despite accelerated sales that have pushed EV share of vehicle market up 3.4% last month to 26.1% (all-time high).
posts.summary: Goldman Sachs projects that accelerating EV adoption will reduce global oil demand by 130,000-320,000 barrels per day by the end of 2027, even as electric vehicles reach record market penetration of 26.1%. The impact represents a meaningful but modest constraint on oil demand growth relative to the scale of global petroleum consumption.

posts.text:
We have more insight than ever. So why are decisions getting harder? 

Markets are more volatile. Decisions more complex. The cost of delay is higher than ever. 

The challenge isn’t knowing more. It’s acting, quickly and with clarity. 

CERA Titan: https://t.co/vhIqgYP6tU https://t.co/lt4iZvjVMw
posts.summary: S&P Global Energy highlights that despite unprecedented market insight, energy sector decision-makers face increased volatility and complexity that demands faster action to mitigate the growing costs of delays. The core challenge is translating information into swift, decisive strategy rather than simply accumulating more data.

posts.text:
Stay ahead of #HurricaneSeason with our revamped Interactive U.S. Gulf Coast Refinery & Hurricane Path Map! Track storms, receive instant alerts, and safeguard your supply chain from #GulfCoast disruptions. Register for free access: https://t.co/ykpjmZmczV

#refinery https://t.co/xwCw34F1rd
posts.summary: OPIS has launched an updated interactive map tracking U.S. Gulf Coast refineries and hurricane paths to help supply chain stakeholders monitor potential disruptions during hurricane season. The tool offers real-time alerts to help mitigate risks to Gulf Coast refining operations, a critical component of U.S. crude oil processing and petroleum product supply.
